name: Ksamil Beaches

description: Turquoise coves and swim-to islets have made Ksamil the star of the fast-rising Albanian Riviera.

description: Dhërmi's lively neighbour — a long turquoise strand of beach clubs and pines beneath the Ceraunian Mountains.

description: Albania's busiest beach — a long, warm, shallow Adriatic strand of resorts stretching south from the ancient port of Durrës.

description: Hidden where a deep canyon meets the Ionian, Gjipe is the wild heart of the Albanian Riviera.

description: The Albanian Riviera's longest beach — seven quiet kilometres of pebble and clear water beneath olive groves and a castle.

sand: White pebbles

description: A compact turquoise cove between Dhërmi and Himarë, a favourite of the young Albanian-Riviera crowd.

description: A brilliant turquoise bay of white pebbles on the Riviera between Sarandë and Himarë, still wild and undeveloped.

description: Himarë's big easy bay — a kilometre of white pebbles and clear turquoise under the olive terraces.

setting: Below the hillside old village of Qeparo, between olive groves and the sea south of Himarë
description: A long, quiet Riviera beach below Qeparo's hillside stone village, backed by olive groves and clear turquoise water.

name: Shëngjin Beach

description: Northern Albania's big sandy resort beach — a long warm-water strand of hotels and bars beside the Kune-Vain lagoons.

description: A vast, shallow family beach of fine sand near the Buna river delta — one of northern Albania's most popular summer resorts.

description: Bosnia and Herzegovina's entire coastline in one town — a sheltered, warm Adriatic channel behind the Pelješac peninsula.

description: Belgium's most elegant seaside resort spreads an enormously wide beach beneath chic villas and galleries.

description: Belgium's widest beach — a vast flat expanse of firm sand beneath big dunes, the birthplace of sand-yachting.

sand: Wide golden sand
swimming: Shallow and gentle, lifeguarded in summer
setting: The lively city beach of Belgium's 'Queen of Coastal Resorts'
description: The broad city beach of Ostend, the 'Queen of the Belgian Coast', backed by a grand promenade and casino.

description: The sand really is golden — a deep, fine strand beneath the forested hills of a Black Sea nature park.

description: Bulgaria's bohemian escape — a long, protected strand where the Balkan range slides into the Black Sea.

swimming: Deep and clear — excellent snorkelling; a sea cave (Cova dels Arcs) opens at one end
setting: A dramatic cove at the foot of the Benitatxell cliffs between Moraira and Jávea
description: A pebble cove trapped beneath sheer cliffs near Benitatxell, with a flooded sea cave at one end.

name: Cala Galdana
sand: Fine white-gold sand curving in a near-perfect horseshoe
swimming: Very sheltered and shallow — one of Menorca's easiest family swims
setting: A pine-rimmed horseshoe bay where the Algendar gorge meets the sea, backed by low-key resort hotels
description: Menorca's grand horseshoe — shallow turquoise water ringed by pine cliffs and easy comforts.

name: Cala Granadella
sand: White pebbles and coarse gravel in a tight crescent
water: Gin-clear turquoise-green over rock and posidonia
swimming: Sheltered and superb for snorkelling; deepens quickly, busy with kayaks by noon
setting: A pine-wrapped notch in the cliffs south of Jávea; the lane down closes once the tiny car park fills
description: The Costa Blanca's snorkel cove — a pine-framed slit of transparent water and a perennial best-beach poll winner.

name: Cala Mitjana
sand: Soft white sand pocketed between pale limestone walls
water: Luminous turquoise over a white seabed
swimming: Clear and calm; ledges around the cove tempt cliff jumpers
setting: A twenty-minute pine-wood walk from Cala Galdana, with no facilities beyond the trees
description: Cala Galdana's wild little sister — white sand and glowing turquoise at the end of a pine path.

name: Cala Mondrago
sand: Soft golden-white sand
swimming: Calm and shallow, ideal for families
setting: A protected natural park of pines and low cliffs
description: A pair of soft turquoise coves cradled by a protected natural park on Mallorca's southeast coast.

name: Cala Turqueta
sand: Fine white sand
swimming: Warm, shallow and calm in the sheltered inlet
setting: A pine-framed cove reached on foot through Menorca's protected south coast
description: Menorca's signature cove — a horseshoe of white sand and glowing turquoise water framed by Aleppo pines.

name: Caló des Moro
sand: Fine white sand
swimming: Deep, clear and calm between steep cliffs
setting: A tiny cliff-walled cove down a steep scramble on Mallorca's south-east
description: Mallorca's most photographed cove, a tiny cliff-cupped scoop of white sand and electric turquoise reached down a rocky scramble.

name: El Golfo (Charco Verde)
sand: Black volcanic sand
swimming: The green lagoon is protected; swim on the adjacent black beach
setting: A half-sunk volcanic crater and a vivid green lagoon on Lanzarote's west coast
description: A vivid emerald lagoon cupped in a half-collapsed volcanic crater, ringed by black sand and rust-red cliffs.

name: Es Trenc
sand: Pale white sand
swimming: Shallow, warm and calm along a long undeveloped strand
setting: Mallorca's longest natural beach, backed by dunes, pines and salt pans
description: Mallorca's great undeveloped beach — three kilometres of white sand and Caribbean shallows backed by dunes and salt flats.

name: Platja de Migjorn
sand: Fine white sand
swimming: Warm, shallow and calm the length of the island's south shore
setting: Formentera's long south-coast beach of dunes and beach bars
description: Formentera's endless south shore, kilometres of white sand and luminous shallows dotted with barefoot beach bars.

name: Playa de Benijo
sand: Black volcanic sand and boulders
swimming: Strong waves and currents, caution advised
setting: The jagged Anaga massif and offshore rock islets
description: A wild black-sand beach beneath the primeval Anaga mountains, famed for its silhouetted rock stacks at sunset.

name: Playa de Bolonia
sand: Broad white Atlantic sand with a colossal dune
swimming: Open Atlantic, breezy, watch for currents
setting: A 30m shifting dune and the Roman ruins of Baelo Claudia
description: A wild Atlantic sweep backed by a towering sand dune and the ruins of a Roman town.

name: Playa de Calblanque
sand: Dark-gold sand below crumbling fossil dunes
water: Clear blue, often with a light shorebreak
swimming: Open-sea feel; lovely on calm days, punchy when the levante blows
surf: Small windswell waves on easterly days
setting: Inside a protected regional park of fossil dunes and arid hills; summer access is by shuttle bus from Los Belones
description: The Costa Cálida before the cranes came — dark-gold sand and fossil dunes in a jealously guarded park.

name: Playa de Cofete
sand: Endless wild golden sand
swimming: Dangerous currents, not for swimming
setting: A 12km beach below the towering Jandia massif
description: A vast, untamed 12-kilometre strand beneath brooding mountains, reached only by a rough dirt track.

name: Playa de El Palmar
sand: A long, straight band of golden sand backed by low dunes and farmland
swimming: Fine between the flags on small days; rips form near the peaks when swell runs
surf: Andalusia's most consistent beach break — mellow peaks, best on autumn and winter swells
setting: A low-rise strip of surf schools and sunset chiringuitos on the Costa de la Luz, still fronted by fields
description: Andalusia's surf village — an unbroken sandy beach break with chiringuito sunsets and no high-rises.

name: Playa de Famara
sand: Golden sand
swimming: Strong currents — better for surfing than swimming
surf: Consistent Atlantic beach-break, Lanzarote's surf heartland
setting: A vast beach beneath a 600-metre cliff wall on Lanzarote's north-west
description: A wild, wind-scoured strand under a towering cliff wall — the raw Atlantic surf capital of Lanzarote.

name: Playa de Gulpiyuri
sand: A small crescent of fine white sand
water: Cool and clear, fed by the sea through caves
swimming: Shallow tidal pool, calmest at high tide
setting: An inland meadow 100m from the open sea
description: A tiny inland beach set in a green meadow, filled by the sea through underground caves.

name: Playa de la Barrosa
sand: Kilometres of fine pale-gold sand, vast at low tide
water: Clean mid-blue Atlantic, brisker than the Med
swimming: Long shallow shelf with playful rollers; well lifeguarded in season
surf: Gentle beach break for bodyboards and beginner foamies
setting: Dunes and pine woods behind the Novo end, with the island castle of Sancti Petri floating off the north tip
description: Cádiz's summer favourite — miles of firm golden sand gazing at the castle islet of Sancti Petri.

name: Playa de la Concha (El Cotillo)
sand: Pale golden sand in a scallop-shaped bay
water: Calm, clear turquoise inside a sheltering reef
swimming: Calm and shallow — a natural lagoon pool, the safest swim on breezy Fuerteventura
setting: A sheltered lagoon held behind rock bars on the wild north-west coast at El Cotillo
description: A scallop of turquoise held calm behind a reef at El Cotillo — the sheltered swim on breezy Fuerteventura.

name: Playa de Las Canteras
sand: Golden sand
water: Calm, clear blue behind a natural reef
swimming: Calm and shallow, sheltered by a natural offshore rock bar
surf: Gentle waves at the reef's end
setting: Las Palmas's three-kilometre urban beach behind the La Barra reef
description: One of Europe's finest city beaches — a golden arc protected by a natural reef along the promenade of Las Palmas.

name: Playa de las Teresitas
sand: Golden Saharan sand, shipped in by the tonne
water: Calm and clear, protected by a breakwater
swimming: Very calm and safe, a true family beach
setting: A palm-lined bay below the Anaga mountains
description: Tenerife's golden showpiece, a palm-lined bay of Saharan sand sheltered by an offshore breakwater.

name: Playa de los Genoveses
sand: Fine golden sand
swimming: Shallow, warm and calm in the wide protected bay
setting: A wild dune-backed bay in the volcanic Cabo de Gata desert park
description: A broad, undeveloped bay of golden sand and clear shallows ringed by the volcanic hills of Cabo de Gata's desert park.

name: Playa de los Muertos
sand: A mile of polished white-grey pebbles sloping steeply to the sea
water: Glassy, gin-clear blue — among Spain's clearest
swimming: Superb clarity but a sharp drop-off and slapping shorebreak; no lifeguards
setting: Below cliffs south of Carboneras on the Cabo de Gata coast, a steep fifteen-minute path down from the viewpoint car parks
description: Almería's mile of white gravel and glass-clear sea, kept honest by the climb back up.

name: Playa de Papagayo
sand: Fine golden sand
swimming: Calm and clear in a sheltered cove
setting: The protected Los Ajaches volcanic cliffs
description: A string of sheltered golden coves and turquoise water tucked into Lanzarote's protected southern cliffs.

name: Playa de Rodiles
sand: A wide fawn-sand sweep at the mouth of the Villaviciosa estuary
swimming: Good bathing off the main sand in summer; keep clear of the estuary channel's tidal currents
surf: Asturias' marquee wave — a fast river-mouth left that lights up on autumn swells
setting: Eucalyptus and pine groves with boardwalk paths inside the Ría de Villaviciosa reserve
description: Asturias' surf temple — a river-mouth left peeling off the Villaviciosa estuary under eucalyptus woods.

name: Playa de Sotavento
sand: Pale golden sand
water: Clear turquoise over a vast lagoon at high tide
swimming: Shallow lagoon at high tide; strong wind and currents otherwise
surf: World-class windsurf and kite spot; big open Atlantic swell
setting: A colossal dune-and-lagoon beach on Fuerteventura's south-east
description: A colossal Atlantic beach where a shallow turquoise lagoon forms at high tide — one of the world's great windsurfing arenas.

name: Playa de Valdevaqueros
sand: Pale golden sand and a giant dune
swimming: Strong Levante wind and currents; a kiter's beach
surf: Europe's premier kitesurf and windsurf beach on the Strait of Gibraltar
setting: A vast dune and windswept sands with Africa on the horizon
description: A windswept Atlantic strand and towering dune at Europe's tip, where kitesurfers fly with Morocco across the strait.

name: Playa de Zahara de los Atunes
sand: A broad, wild ribbon of golden Atlantic sand
swimming: Open and breezy with gentle shelving; levante wind some days
setting: An old tuna-fishing village on the windswept Costa de la Luz
description: The Costa de la Luz at its purest — a wild golden ribbon beside an old tuna-fishing village.

name: Playa del Duque
sand: Fine pale-golden sand, groomed daily below the five-star terraces
swimming: Sheltered and gently shelving, with lifeguards and flags in season
setting: Costa Adeje's most polished bay, lined with thatched parasols, boardwalk boutiques and the Bahía del Duque hotel
description: Tenerife's most manicured beach — blond sand and straw parasols beneath the Bahía del Duque.

name: Playa del Postiguet
sand: Soft golden sand in the heart of the city
swimming: Calm, shallow and lifeguarded — Alicante's easy city swim
setting: Alicante's palm-lined city beach directly below the Santa Bárbara castle
description: Alicante's golden city beach, curving below the Santa Bárbara castle beside a palm-lined promenade.

name: Playa del Sardinero
sand: A grand sweep of fine golden sand
swimming: Lively but guarded; belle-époque bathing tradition
setting: Santander's belle-époque seafront of casinos and gardens
description: Royal-era Santander on parade — a grand golden city beach below the casino and gardens of El Sardinero.

name: Playa del Silencio
sand: White pebbles and coarse sand
swimming: Chilly, rocky entry and no services
setting: A curved amphitheatre of green cliffs and offshore rock needles
description: A silent, cliff-ringed amphitheatre of pebbles and sea stacks on Asturias' wild Cantabrian coast.

name: Praia da Lanzada
sand: Two kilometres of fine white Atlantic sand
swimming: Bracing but gentle on calm days; a fertility-rite chapel at one end
setting: A dune-backed arc on the Rías Baixas with a 12th-century hermitage
description: Galicia's great white arc — two kilometres of cold clean Atlantic beside a hermitage of fertility legends.

name: Sa Calobra (Torrent de Pareis)
sand: Pale pebbles and coarse sand at the canyon mouth
swimming: Startlingly clear and quickly deep; dangerous when north swells push in
setting: Where Mallorca's greatest gorge splits the Tramuntana, reached through rock tunnels from Sa Calobra hamlet or by boat from Port de Sóller
description: The Tramuntana's dramatic full stop — a pebble cove where the Torrent de Pareis canyon empties into sapphire sea.

name: Tossa de Mar
sand: Coarse golden sand
swimming: Sheltered and calm below the walled headland
setting: A crescent beneath a medieval walled old town and its clifftop castle
description: A golden crescent beneath the honey-coloured medieval walls and clifftop tower that make Tossa the Costa Brava's icon.

name: Zarautz
sand: Golden sand
swimming: Lively Atlantic swell; lifeguarded swimming zones
surf: The Basque coast's longest beach-break and a surf-town institution
setting: The Basque Country's longest beach along a lively surf-and-pintxo town
description: The Basque coast's longest beach — a golden surf strand fronting a promenade of pintxo bars and board shops.

name: Cala Benirràs
sand: Coarse golden sand and pebbles
swimming: Sheltered and clear in the deep-cut bay
setting: A cliff-ringed northern bay famous for Sunday sunset drum circles
description: Ibiza's bohemian heart, a cliff-ringed northern cove where crowds gather each Sunday to drum the sun down.

name: Cala Pregonda
sand: Reddish-gold sand
swimming: Sheltered and calm behind offshore rock islets
setting: A Mars-red cove on Menorca's wild north coast, reached on a long walk
description: A wild, Mars-red cove on Menorca's rugged north coast, its ochre rock islets rising from clear green water.

name: Cala Salada
sand: Golden sand with rocky edges
swimming: Calm and sheltered bay
setting: Red cliffs, pines and old fishermen's boat huts
description: A sheltered emerald cove ringed by red cliffs and old fishermen's huts north of Sant Antoni.

name: Platja de Castell
sand: Coarse golden sand
swimming: Clean and calm in the sheltered bay
setting: One of the Costa Brava's last undeveloped bays, saved by referendum
description: The Costa Brava's great unspoilt bay — pine-backed sand and hidden coves that locals voted to save from developers.

name: Playa de Cuevas del Mar
sand: Pale sand threaded between eroded rock arches
swimming: Sheltered pools at low tide between the arches
setting: A cove of wave-carved arches and caves on the Llanes coast
description: A geology lesson you can swim in — arches, tunnels and caves carved through an Asturian cove.

name: Playa de Güigüí
locality: La Aldea de San Nicolás, Gran Canaria
sand: Dark volcanic grey-gold sand, broadest at low tide
swimming: Open-ocean swell and shore dump; only sensible on calm days
setting: A roadless west-coast nature reserve reached by a three-hour ridge hike from Tasartico or by boat from La Aldea
description: Gran Canaria's last wild shore — dark sand under kilometre-high cliffs, earned by boot or boat.

name: Playa de Laga
sand: Fine golden sand beneath the Ogoño headland
swimming: Swim the sheltered corner; the middle catches swell
setting: The Urdaibai biosphere reserve, with the great Ogoño cliff at one end
description: The Basque coast in one frame — golden sand, green hills and surf beneath the mighty Ogoño headland.

name: Playa de las Conchas
sand: Golden sand flecked with shells
swimming: Strong currents, swimming discouraged
setting: Beneath red Montana Bermeja, facing wild islets
description: A dazzling turquoise-and-gold beach on car-free La Graciosa, wild and windswept below a red volcano.

name: Playa de Torimbia
sand: Fine pale sand
swimming: Cool and lively; some current on the open shell-shaped bay
setting: An undeveloped shell-shaped bay reached on a cliff path in green Asturias
description: A perfect undeveloped shell of pale sand cupped by green Asturian cliffs, reached only on foot down a headland path.

name: Praia de Carnota
sand: Pale golden sand
swimming: Cold and lively with Atlantic currents
setting: Galicia's longest beach, a wild seven-kilometre sweep of sand, dunes and marsh
description: Galicia's longest beach — a wild seven-kilometre arc of sand, dunes and tidal marsh beneath the green Costa da Morte hills.

name: Hietaniemi Beach
sand: Pale sand
swimming: Shallow, calm and briefly summer-warm
setting: Helsinki's beloved 'Hietsu' city beach with beach volleyball and a café
description: Helsinki's beloved 'Hietsu' — the capital's summer heart of sand, beach volleyball and long Nordic evenings.

name: Kalajoki Hiekkasärkät
sand: Wide golden dunes rolling into the Gulf of Bothnia
water: Shallow, surprisingly warm Bothnian water in July
swimming: Very shallow — wade far out over rippled sand
setting: Finland's great dune coast, a resort town on the Gulf of Bothnia
description: Golden dunes at 64° north — Finland's improbable beach resort where the Bothnian shallows warm under the midnight sun.

name: Nallikari Beach
sand: A long, gently curving sweep of fine pale sand
water: Nearly fresh — the Bothnian Bay is barely brackish — and quick to warm in July
swimming: An endless shallow wade; among the gentlest swims this far north
setting: On Hietasaari island at Oulu's edge, spa hotel behind the dunes; nightless nights in June, sea ice by January
description: Oulu's Riviera of the North — shallow, almost-fresh water and white-night bathing at 65°N.

name: Plagen Beach
sand: Soft pale sand between smooth granite outcrops
swimming: Gentle and shallow; Finland's warmest corner, relatively speaking
setting: Finland's sunny southern spa town of ornate wooden villas
description: Finland's riviera — pale sand and smooth granite below the ornate wooden villas of sunny Hanko.

name: Yyteri Beach
sand: Fine pale sand and dunes
swimming: Very shallow and gently warming; wade far out
setting: Finland's largest sandy beach, a six-kilometre dune strand on the Gulf of Bothnia
description: Finland's largest beach — six kilometres of dunes and sand so shallow you can wade far into the Gulf of Bothnia.

name: Tjornuvik
sand: Dark grey-black sand in a bay at the island's northern end
swimming: For cold-water surfers and hardy swimmers only
surf: A noted Faroese cold-water surf break
setting: A tiny turf-roofed village ringed by peaks, facing the Risin og Kellingin sea stacks
description: Black sand in an amphitheatre of peaks, gazing out at the Faroes' famous giant-and-witch sea stacks.

name: Dune du Pilat
sand: Vast pale sand at the foot of Europe's tallest dune
water: Cool Atlantic at the mouth of Arcachon Bay
swimming: Strong currents near the Banc d'Arguin channel, caution advised
setting: A 100-metre wall of sand and pine forest above, the Banc d'Arguin sandbank offshore
description: The beach at the foot of Europe's tallest sand dune, a hundred-metre mountain of sand sliding into the Atlantic.

name: La Graviere
sand: Golden sand with a steep shorebreak and shifting banks
swimming: Dangerous, heavy shorebreak and rips, strong swimmers only
surf: World-class barreling beach break, host of pro-tour events
setting: Open Landes dune coast lined with pine forest
description: One of the planet's finest beach-break barrels, drawing the pro surf tour to Hossegor's shifting sandbanks each autumn.

name: Nice Beach
sand: Smooth grey pebbles (galets) — no sand, a mat helps
water: Startlingly blue and clear for a big city; deepens fast
swimming: Shelves steeply into deep water; watch the shore-dump when swell runs
setting: Seven kilometres of open shingle below the Promenade des Anglais, Belle Époque hotels at your back
description: The Riviera's great city beach — grey pebbles, improbably blue water and the Promenade des Anglais above.

name: Plage de la Cote des Basques
sand: Dark golden sand that vanishes at high tide
swimming: Only at low to mid tide, strong currents and disappearing beach
surf: Legendary longboard break, birthplace of European surfing
setting: Backed by cliffs and Belle Epoque villas with views to the Spanish Pyrenees
description: The cradle of European surfing, a cliff-framed Atlantic beach where longboarders have carved swell since the 1950s.

name: Plage de Palombaggia
sand: Pale gold
swimming: Warm, shallow, calm
setting: Umbrella pines & red rocks
description: Umbrella pines and red granite framing Corsica’s dreamiest sands.

name: Plage de Pampelonne
sand: Golden
swimming: Calm, warm
setting: Legendary beach-club strip
description: Five kilometres of Riviera glamour and the original celebrity beach clubs.

name: Calanque d'En-Vau
sand: Small white-pebble and coarse-sand cove
swimming: Calm and sheltered, deep and cool
setting: Towering white limestone cliffs of the Calanques National Park
description: A cathedral of sheer white limestone walls plunging into emerald water, reached only on foot or by boat.

name: Calanque de Sormiou
sand: Pale sand and pebbles
swimming: Deep and clear beneath the white limestone
setting: A fjord-like limestone inlet in the Calanques National Park
description: A fjord-like limestone inlet of jade water and pale cliffs in the Calanques park, reached on foot or by boat from Marseille.

name: Étretat Beach
sand: White pebble
swimming: Cold, pebbly
setting: Chalk cliffs & arches
description: The chalk arches and needle that Monet painted again and again.

name: Grande Plage
sand: Golden
swimming: Patrolled, rips
setting: Belle-Époque resort
description: The elegant Basque resort where European surfing began.

name: Grande Plage des Sables-d'Olonne
sand: Fine pale-gold sand, enormous at low tide
water: Clean green-blue Atlantic, calmer than most of this coast
swimming: Gently shelving, sheltered by the harbour channel and patrolled all summer — a safe family swim
surf: Usually small; surfers head around the corner to Tanchet
setting: A perfect town-hugging crescent under the Remblai promenade of the Vendée Globe port
description: The Vendée Globe town's front garden — a safe golden crescent beneath the Remblai promenade.

name: Omaha Beach
sand: Wide flat golden sand
swimming: Shallow and calm at high tide; vast flats at low tide
setting: A vast, solemn D-Day landing beach backed by memorials and a cemetery
description: A vast, wind-swept strand of huge tides and deep history — the most storied of the 1944 D-Day landing beaches.

name: Paloma Beach
sand: Pebble-and-shingle cove, part public and part private club
swimming: Calm, clear and sheltered in the anse
setting: The glamorous Cap Ferrat peninsula, a chic beach club and villa-lined shore
description: A chic Riviera cove on billionaires' Cap Ferrat, where a celebrated beach club meets clear Mediterranean blue.

name: Plage d'Argelès-sur-Mer
sand: Seven kilometres of fine grey-gold sand, wide and gently sloped
swimming: Long shallow entry with supervised posts all summer — built for children
surf: None; windsurf chop when the tramontane blows
setting: Europe's campsite capital, a pine-lined promenade at the front and the Albères dropping the Pyrenees into the sea just south
description: The Roussillon family shore — seven gentle kilometres where the Pyrenees finally meet the Med.

name: Plage de Biscarrosse
sand: Immense flat apron of pale golden sand, dunes to the horizon both ways
swimming: Baïnes (rip channels) shift daily — swim only in the supervised zones
surf: Reliable sandbar peaks; one of the Landes' great surf-school towns
setting: Where the Landes pine forest simply stops at the dune line; seaplane lakes just behind
description: A vast Landes strand where the pines end, the dunes begin and the sandbars peel all summer.

name: Plage de Calvi
sand: Soft light-gold sand curving four kilometres under an unbroken umbrella-pine fringe
water: Glass-clear turquoise shading to deep blue across the gulf
swimming: Shallow, calm and warm — one of Corsica's easiest family swims
setting: The Gulf of Calvi below the Genoese citadel, beach bars hidden in the pinède, granite peaks behind
description: Four pine-shaded golden kilometres curving away from Calvi's Genoese citadel.

name: Plage de l'Almanarre
sand: Narrow coarse pale sand banked with dried posidonia seagrass
water: Clear, shallow aquamarine over sand and eelgrass meadows
swimming: Warm shallow entry along four kilometres; choppy and kite-crowded when the mistral blows
surf: No waves — but a world-class flat-water kitesurf and windsurf arena in a mistral
setting: The western arm of the Giens double tombolo, salt pans and flamingo lagoons just behind the road
description: France's wind-sports mecca — a tombolo strand where mistral days fill the sky with kites.

name: Plage de Lacanau
sand: Broad blond Atlantic sand backed by high grass-tufted dunes
water: Cool jade-green Atlantic, white with surf most days
swimming: Powerful waves and baïne rip currents — bathe between the flags in summer
surf: Consistent beach-break peaks for all levels; the Lacanau Pro has run here since 1979
setting: A laid-back surf town in the Médoc pines, an hour due west of Bordeaux
description: Bordeaux's ocean — a dune-backed surf beach that has hosted the Lacanau Pro since 1979.

name: Plage de Notre-Dame
sand: Fine pale golden sand
swimming: Calm, shallow and gentle
setting: Umbrella-pine forest inside a protected national park on a car-free island
description: Often called France's most beautiful beach, a turquoise arc backed by umbrella pines on car-free Porquerolles.

name: Plage de Rondinara
sand: Fine white sand in a near-circular bay
swimming: Calm, warm and shallow, family friendly
setting: Almost-enclosed shell-shaped bay between Bonifacio and Porto-Vecchio
description: A near-perfect circular bay of shallow turquoise water, curved like a scallop shell in Corsica's deep south.

name: Plage de Saint-Nicolas
sand: Blinding white shell-and-maerl sand, fine as sugar
water: Genuinely tropical-looking turquoise over a white bottom — in Brittany
swimming: Gin-clear and brisk; calmest in the lagoon-like La Chambre anchorage between the isles
setting: Car-free islet of the Glénan archipelago ten miles off Fouesnant, seasonal boats from Bénodet and Concarneau
description: The 'Breton Tahiti' — white shell-sand and turquoise shallows ten miles off the Finistère coast.

name: Plage de Santa Giulia
sand: Fine white sand
swimming: Warm, shallow and calm in the near-enclosed bay
setting: A sheltered lagoon-like bay of umbrella pines in southern Corsica
description: A near-enclosed lagoon of luminous turquoise shallows and white sand, southern Corsica's most tropical bay.

name: Plage de Trestraou
locality: Perros-Guirec, Cote de Granit Rose, Brittany
sand: Long crescent of fine pale sand
water: Cool clear Atlantic with a big tidal range
swimming: Refreshing to cold, gentle when sheltered, large tides
setting: Gateway to the Pink Granite Coast and its rose-hued boulder formations
description: A sheltered Breton crescent that launches the coastal path past the surreal rose-pink granite boulders of Perros-Guirec.

name: Plage du Sillon
sand: Three kilometres of firm golden sand below a great sea wall
water: Jade-green Channel water with huge tides
swimming: Guarded bathing; at spring tides the waves explode over the wall
setting: The corsair city's ramparts and breakwater timbers
description: Saint-Malo's grand strand — three kilometres of tide-swept sand beneath the corsair city's ramparts.

name: Deauville Beach
sand: Wide pale sand
swimming: Shallow and gentle, lifeguarded in summer
setting: The grand Belle-Époque resort of parasols, boardwalk and beach cabanas
description: Normandy's grande-dame resort — a wide sand beach of coloured parasols, a wooden boardwalk and film-festival glamour.

name: Hendaye Plage
sand: Three kilometres of gentle golden sand
water: Clean Atlantic blue, softer here than anywhere on the Basque coast
swimming: The Basque coast's safest family swimming
surf: France's best beginner waves — soft, long and forgiving
setting: The last French beach before Spain, facing the Deux Jumeaux rocks
description: France's gentlest surf beach — long soft waves on the last sand before the Spanish border.

name: Plage d'Argent
sand: Silver-white sand that named the beach
swimming: Calm, shallow and clear off a car-free island
setting: Pine forest on the national-park island of Porquerolles
description: Silver sand and pool-clear shallows on car-free Porquerolles — Provence's national-park island.

name: Plage de l'Espiguette
sand: Vast pale sand and dunes
swimming: Shallow, warm and calm along an endless open strand
setting: A huge wild dune beach on the edge of the Camargue delta
description: A vast, wild sweep of pale sand and shifting dunes on the flamingo-filled edge of the Camargue.

name: Plage de l'Ostriconi
sand: Golden sand and dunes
swimming: Clear and lively where a river meets the sea
surf: Wind swell on the open bay
setting: A wild dune-and-river beach at the gateway to Corsica's Désert des Agriates
description: A wild golden beach where a river winds through dunes into turquoise sea, guarding the edge of Corsica's Agriates desert.

name: Plage de la Conche des Baleines
sand: A vast, dune-backed crescent of pale sand

setting: A wild cedar-grove beach with no facilities beside Elafonissi
description: A wild, facility-free 'cedar forest' beach where junipers grow to the edge of turquoise shallows next to Elafonissi.

name: Kolimbithra Beach
sand: Two soft-sand coves split by a headland
water: Clear Aegean blue with meltemi waves
swimming: The small cove is calm; the big one catches the wind
surf: The Cyclades' surf spot — meltemi swell all summer
setting: Tinos's green north side, dovecotes and reed beds behind the sand
description: The Cyclades' unlikely surf beach — meltemi waves rolling into Tinos's green northern bay.

name: Mylopotas Beach
sand: Fine golden sand
swimming: Warm, shallow and calm in the wide sheltered bay
setting: A long resort bay below Ios town, the island's watersports and party hub
description: Ios's big golden party bay — a long crescent of sand and calm turquoise water lined with beach bars and watersports.

name: Pori Beach
sand: Wide horseshoe of fine pale sand
water: Impossibly clear turquoise, shallow far offshore
swimming: Warm, calm and wadeable — among the clearest bays in the Cyclades
setting: A sweeping remote bay on tiny, car-light Koufonisi
description: A curving bay of glassy turquoise on the sleepy Small Cyclades island of Koufonisi.

name: Seychelles Beach
sand: Smooth white marble-like pebbles and coarse pale sand
water: Piercing turquoise over a white stone bed
swimming: Brilliantly clear and quickly deep, sheltered by the cliff walls
setting: A cove born of a landslide during road-building, a short scramble down from the coast road or a boat ride from Manganitis
description: Ikaria's accidental jewel — a landslide-made cove of white pebbles and Seychelles-blue water near Manganitis.

name: St Paul's Bay
sand: Fine sand and pebbles
swimming: Sheltered, calm and clear in the near-enclosed bay
setting: A near-circular cove below the Lindos acropolis where St Paul is said to have landed
description: A near-enclosed turquoise cove beneath the ancient Lindos acropolis, where St Paul is said to have come ashore.

name: Stafylos Beach
sand: Pale pebbles and sand in a pine-ringed cove
swimming: Sheltered and clear beneath the pines
setting: A pine-wrapped Sporades cove named for a Minoan king's grave
description: Skopelos's pine-wrapped classic — clear green water in the cove where a Minoan king was buried.

name: Xigia Beach
sand: Pebbles below cliffs
swimming: Cool and buoyant; natural sulphur springs said to soften the skin
setting: A tiny cliff-backed cove with natural sulphur springs on northern Zakynthos
description: A tiny cliff-cupped cove where natural sulphur springs turn the water a milky blue said to be good for the skin.

name: Punta Rata
sand: White pebbles and shingle
swimming: Clean, gently deepening
setting: Pine-shaded promenade below the Biokovo mountains, with the offshore Kamen Brela rock
description: Croatia's poster beach, a pine-fringed pebble shore below the Biokovo massif with a lone islet offshore.

name: Zlatni Rat
sand: White pebble
swimming: Clear, deep edges
setting: Shifting sand horn
description: The “Golden Horn” — a shifting pebble spit that changes with the wind.

name: Bačvice Beach
sand: Rare natural sand, very shallow
swimming: Famously shallow and warm — you can wade far out
setting: Split's sandy city beach, home of the local paddle-ball game picigin
description: Split's much-loved sandy city bay, so shallow locals play their splashy picigin ball game far from shore.

name: Banje Beach
sand: Fine pebbles and imported sand
swimming: Good and lively
setting: Beach club just outside the Old Town walls with views to Lokrum island
description: Dubrovnik's classic pebble beach, gazing back at the Old Town walls and Lokrum island.

name: Bijeca Beach
sand: A long, rare-for-Istria stretch of fine sand, shallow and gently shelving
water: Warm, shallow and clear in a sheltered bay
swimming: Very shallow and warm — you can wade far out; ideal for families
setting: Istria's best sand beach, curving along a sheltered bay at Medulin near the peninsula's southern tip
description: Istria is a coast of pebbles, so Medulin's long shallow sand bay is the peninsula's family favourite.

name: Kraljičina Plaža
sand: Fine pale sand in a 3 km arc — a rarity on this coast
water: Warm milky-turquoise shallows over rippled sand
swimming: Waist-deep for a long way out; one of the safest family swims in Dalmatia
surf: None; steady lagoon winds draw kitesurf schools instead
setting: A sandy spit fringing the Nin lagoon, facing the full Velebit massif, with Croatia's largest medicinal-mud site just behind
description: Often called Croatia's longest sandy beach — a warm lagoon spit with healing mud and a royal Velebit panorama.

name: Mulini Beach
sand: Landscaped pebble terraces and smooth stone platforms
swimming: Clear and calm off the stone platforms, deepening quickly
setting: Rovinj's chic beach-club shore, a short walk from the old town through the Zlatni Rt pine park
description: Rovinj's stylish shore — landscaped pebble terraces and beach bars a short walk from the old town.

name: Palmižana
sand: Part pebble, part sand, under pines, rosemary and agaves
water: Aquamarine, bright over a pale seabed
swimming: Sheltered south-facing cove; clear and calm, busy with tender traffic by noon
setting: Vinogradišće cove on Sveti Klement, ringed by the Meneghello family's century-old arboretum and famed bay restaurants; taxi boats run 20 minutes from Hvar town
description: The Pakleni Islands' bohemian cove — arboretum greenery, aquamarine water and long seafood lunches above the sand.

name: Paradise Beach (Rajska Plaža)
sand: A rare Adriatic expanse of real fine sand
swimming: Knee-deep for hundreds of metres — perfect for small children
setting: Rab island's famous sandy bay ringed by low pine headlands
description: Real sand in a pebble-and-rock sea — Rab's Paradise Beach stays knee-deep halfway across the bay.

name: Pasjača Beach
sand: Pebbles beneath sheer cliffs
swimming: Deep and clear, dramatic below the rock walls
setting: A cliff-foot cove reached by a steep tunnelled path south of Dubrovnik
description: A pebble strip at the foot of towering cliffs, reached down a rock-cut tunnel — repeatedly voted Europe's best beach.

name: Sakarun
sand: White sand and fine shingle
swimming: Shallow, warm, family-friendly
setting: Pine-backed bay on a remote island
description: A rare sandy Adriatic lagoon whose shallow turquoise water draws comparisons to the Caribbean.

name: Stiniva Cove
sand: White pebble
swimming: Clear, deep
setting: Cliffs pinched to a keyhole
description: A hidden cove where two cliffs nearly meet over a pebble beach.

name: Sveti Ivan Beach
sand: White pebbles in a tiny half-moon
swimming: Deep, brilliantly clear — snorkelling water
setting: A 45-minute scramble below the 4,000-year-old clifftop hamlet of Lubenice
description: A luminous pebble half-moon 378 metres below the ancient hamlet of Lubenice — earn it on foot or arrive by boat.

name: Trstenica
sand: Sand and fine pebble along a kilometre-long crescent
water: Clear and gently shelving, with the Korčula archipelago across the channel
swimming: Big shallow area that suits children; calm on most summer days
setting: Ten minutes' walk east of Orebić's ferry pier beneath the 961 m Sveti Ilija ridge, pines for shade
description: Orebić's big family beach — a sandy kilometre of shallows looking straight at the Korčula archipelago.

name: Vela Plaža
sand: Fine pebbles in an emphatic 1.8 km crescent
water: Clear blue, shallow at the shore then steadily deeper
swimming: Gently shelving entry that suits children and non-swimmers
setting: Fronted by Baška's café promenade, with bare karst ridges rising on both sides of the valley
description: Krk's showpiece — a 1.8 km pebble crescent under bare karst ridges, flying the Blue Flag since 1999.

name: Zrće Beach
sand: White pebbles
swimming: Clear and shallow, warm in the sheltered bay
setting: Croatia's open-air club beach, lined with day-and-night beach bars
description: The 'Croatian Ibiza' — a bright white-pebble bay on Pag that turns into a round-the-clock open-air club all summer.

name: Copacabana Beach
sand: Pebbles and imported sand
swimming: Calm and shallow with watersports and slides
setting: A family resort beach on the Lapad peninsula with Adriatic sunset views
description: Dubrovnik's liveliest resort beach, a west-facing Lapad cove of watersports, floating slides and Adriatic sunsets.

name: Divna Beach
sand: White pebbles
swimming: Clear and calm in the sheltered pine-ringed bay
setting: A remote pine-fringed bay — 'divna' means lovely — on northern Pelješac
description: Aptly named 'lovely', a remote pine-ringed bay of white pebbles and clear turquoise on the wild Pelješac peninsula.

name: Dubovica
sand: White pebbles
swimming: Clear, deepening, good snorkelling
setting: A lone old stone house and terraced hills in an unspoilt cove
description: A secluded pebble cove on Hvar's south coast framed by a single old stone house and vineyards.

name: Hawaii Beach (Havajke)
sand: White pebbles and rock ledges below low pines
water: Bright, clear turquoise over a pale seabed
swimming: Deep, clear and calm, with ladders off the rocks
setting: A turquoise cove nicknamed 'Hawaii' on Pula's hotel-lined Verudela peninsula
description: A cove so bright it earned the name 'Hawaii' — clear turquoise below the pines of Pula's Verudela peninsula.

name: Nugal Beach
sand: White pebbles
swimming: Deep, clear and calm in the cliff-backed cove
setting: A secluded cove between Makarska and Tučepi, reached by coastal path or boat
description: A secluded white-pebble cove backed by dramatic cliffs and pines, hidden along the coastal path from Makarska.

name: Oprna Bay
sand: Fine gravel and pale pebbles beneath a steep scree slope
water: Startling turquoise, clear to the seabed from the shore
swimming: Snorkelling-grade clarity; calm mornings, though wind can funnel down the slope
setting: Below the Stara Baška road on Krk's rugged south coast — a steep rocky scramble down, or arrive by boat
description: Often called Krk's most beautiful beach — a scree-backed turquoise cove you earn by a steep scramble.

name: Proizd
sand: White rock shelves
swimming: Deep and glass-clear off the smooth white rocks
setting: A tiny uninhabited islet of white-rock bathing shelves off Korčula
description: A tiny uninhabited islet whose smooth white rock shelves drop into some of the clearest turquoise water in the Adriatic.

name: Rt Kamenjak
sand: Rock shelves and pebble coves
swimming: Deep and crystal-clear off the rocks and in hidden coves
setting: A wild protected cape of dozens of coves at Istria's southern tip
description: Istria's wild southern cape — a protected headland of scrub, dinosaur tracks and dozens of clear-water rock coves.

name: Saplunara Beach
sand: Fine golden sand — 'sabbia' gave the bay its name
swimming: Calm and shallow inside the pine-ringed inlet
setting: The sandy southern tip of green Mljet, Odysseus's island
description: Golden sand on Odysseus's green island — Mljet's sheltered southern inlet, warm and pine-ringed.

name: Srebrna Beach
sand: Large silver-white pebbles polished smooth, flanked by flat stone slabs
swimming: Gradual pebble entry into calm, transparent water
setting: A bare 400 m cove near Rukavac hamlet on Vis's south coast — pine woods behind, no bars or kiosks on the stones
description: Named for its silver-glowing white pebbles — a commerce-free cove of glass-clear water on Vis's south shore.

sand: Rare golden sand
swimming: Shallow, warm and calm — one of the Adriatic's few true sand beaches
setting: A sandy bay on car-free Lopud, reached by walk or golf-buggy from the ferry
description: A rare true sand beach on car-free Lopud, its shallow turquoise water a short walk across the island from the ferry.

name: Vela Pržina
sand: Golden sand
swimming: Warm, shallow and calm
setting: A rare sandy bay among the vineyards of Lumbarda on Korčula
description: A rare golden-sand bay backed by the sandy Grk-wine vineyards of Lumbarda on the island of Korčula.

name: Zavratnica

swimming: Deep, clear and sheltered in the narrow inlet
setting: A slender 800-metre inlet beneath the Velebit mountains, reached on foot
description: A fjord-like inlet knifing 800 metres into the Velebit massif, its vivid water reached only on foot or by boat.

name: Ballymastocker Bay
sand: A golden two-kilometre crescent once voted among the world's most beautiful
swimming: Sheltered by the lough — cold but calm
setting: The green flank of the Fanad peninsula on Lough Swilly
description: Donegal's golden crescent on Lough Swilly — regularly ranked among the world's most beautiful beaches.

name: Inch Beach
sand: A five-kilometre sand spit reaching out into Dingle Bay
swimming: Popular but with rips; lifeguarded in summer
surf: Reliable beach break, a favourite Kerry learner and longboard spot
setting: A long dune-backed spit between the Slieve Mish Mountains and the sea
description: A five-kilometre finger of surf-swept sand pointing into Dingle Bay beneath the Kerry mountains.

name: Keem Bay
sand: Sheltered horseshoe of white-gold sand at the foot of steep cliffs
water: Clear turquoise, unusually calm for the Atlantic
swimming: Sheltered and clear but cold; a Blue Flag beach
setting: Steep green cliffs and a hairpin mountain road at the western tip of Achill Island
description: A turquoise horseshoe cupped by soaring green cliffs at the wild western tip of Achill Island.

name: Strandhill Beach
sand: Dark-gold sand below a bank of storm-rolled cobbles

setting: Under cairn-topped Knocknarea, with seaweed baths and chip vans along the shorefront
description: Sligo's surf capital beneath Queen Maeve's mountain — all waves, no swimming.

name: Banna Strand
sand: Vast golden sand and high dunes
swimming: Broad and lively; lifeguarded in summer
setting: A long dune-backed Kerry strand with Tralee Bay views
description: A long, dune-backed Kerry strand of golden sand and rolling Atlantic surf beneath the Slieve Mish mountains.

name: Barleycove Beach
sand: Pale gold sand between dunes thrown up by the 1755 Lisbon tsunami

setting: A protected dune system near Ireland's southernmost point
description: Dunes raised by the great Lisbon tsunami of 1755 cradle West Cork's loveliest pale-gold bay.

name: Coumeenoole Beach
sand: Golden sand among rocks
swimming: Strong currents — a dramatic look-don't-swim beach
setting: A wild cove below Slea Head, facing the Blasket Islands
description: A wild golden cove beneath the cliffs of Slea Head at Ireland's far south-west, gazing out to the Blasket Islands.

name: Curracloe Beach
sand: Vast pale golden sand
swimming: Shallow and gentle; lifeguarded in summer
setting: An eleven-kilometre dune strand that stood in for Normandy in Saving Private Ryan
description: An eleven-kilometre sweep of pale dune-backed sand in Wexford, famous as the D-Day beach of Saving Private Ryan.

name: Dog's Bay
sand: Brilliant white shell sand
swimming: Cold but clear and sheltered on the near-enclosed bay
setting: A back-to-back white-sand bay of pure ground shell in wild Connemara
description: A dazzling horseshoe of pure white shell sand and turquoise water, a slice of the tropics on Connemara's wild coast.

name: Inchydoney
sand: Wide golden sand
swimming: Gently shelving with reliable surf; lifeguarded
surf: A friendly beach-break split by a rocky headland
setting: A twin golden strand split by a green headland in West Cork
description: A twin sweep of golden sand split by a grassy headland, West Cork's favourite surf and family beach.

name: Reynisfjara Black Sand Beach
sand: Jet-black volcanic
swimming: No — deadly sneaker waves
setting: Basalt columns & sea stacks
description: Black basalt sand and hexagonal columns — beautiful and genuinely dangerous.

name: Diamond Beach
sand: Black volcanic
swimming: No
setting: Icebergs stranded on black sand
description: Glittering chunks of glacier ice scattered like diamonds on black sand.

name: Breiðavík
sand: A broad crescent of blonde sand, startling against dark Westfjords moorland
water: Improbably turquoise over pale shallows on clear days
swimming: Sub-10°C — a quick plunge for the hardy
setting: A lone church and guesthouse behind the bay, on the road out to the Látrabjarg bird cliffs
description: The Westfjords' turquoise surprise — a golden bay at the end of the road to the Látrabjarg puffin cliffs.

name: Djupalonssandur
sand: Smooth black volcanic pebbles rather than sand
swimming: Not for swimming; dangerous surf and sneaker waves
setting: Lava cliffs, rock arches and rusted shipwreck remains beneath Snaefellsjokull glacier
description: Smooth black lava pearls, jagged rock arches and shipwreck iron beneath a glacier-topped volcano.

name: Stokksnes
sand: Jet-black volcanic sand and dunes
swimming: Frigid — a place to photograph, not swim
setting: A black-sand headland mirroring the jagged Vestrahorn massif
description: A black-sand headland where tufted dunes and mirror pools reflect the jagged spires of the Vestrahorn massif.

name: Ytri Tunga
sand: Golden sand and seaweed-draped skerries — a rarity on volcanic Iceland

setting: A farm shore under the Snæfellsnes mountains where harbour seals haul out on the rocks

sand: Imported golden sand

description: Reykjavík's geothermal beach — golden sand imported to a cove where hot spring water warms the North Atlantic enough to swim.

name: Raudasandur
sand: A ten-kilometre sweep of pink-red sand from crushed scallop shells
water: Cold Atlantic across vast shifting tidal flats
swimming: Too cold and remote for real swimming, but wade the shallows
setting: A wild, remote Westfjords bay backed by mountains, reached by a steep gravel road
description: A ten-kilometre sweep of red-gold shell sand that shifts colour with the light in Iceland's remote Westfjords.

name: Cala Goloritzé
sand: White pebble
swimming: Clear, deep
setting: Hike- or boat-only cove
description: A protected cove with a soaring limestone spire, reached on foot or by boat.

name: Cala Luna
sand: Pale golden sand backed by huge shady caves
swimming: Clear and deep-shelving; calm most summer mornings
setting: Reached by boat from Cala Gonone or a two-hour gorge hike, framed by limestone cliffs
description: The Golfo di Orosei's poster cove — pale sand and turquoise water beneath gaping limestone caves.

name: Cala Mariolu
sand: White pebbles and pea-sized shingle
water: Deep sapphire shading to pale turquoise
swimming: Deep, clear, superb snorkelling
setting: Towering Supramonte limestone cliffs, boat access only
description: A boat-only cove of white pebbles beneath soaring cliffs where the sea glows in bands of blue.

name: Chia (Su Giudeu)
sand: Fine pale sand backed by juniper dunes
swimming: Warm, shallow and calm; wade to the little Su Giudeu islet
surf: Occasional wind swell — a kite and windsurf spot
setting: Dunes, a Spanish watchtower and flamingo lagoons on Sardinia's south coast
description: Southern Sardinia's postcard beach, where juniper dunes frame a turquoise shallows you can wade across to a tiny islet.

name: Fiordo di Furore
sand: Small pebble and coarse-sand pocket
swimming: Deep and dramatic
setting: A narrow ravine spanned by a high arched bridge
description: A sliver of beach hides in a deep coastal ravine beneath the Amalfi Coast's famous arched bridge.

name: Isola Bella
sand: Grey pebbles and shingle
swimming: Deepens quickly, good snorkelling
setting: Tiny nature-reserve islet below Taormina's cliffs
description: A pebble spit links the mainland to a miniature island reserve beneath clifftop Taormina.

name: La Pelosa Beach
sand: Powder white
swimming: Shallow, warm, calm
setting: Watchtower & islet
description: A Caribbean-clear shallow flat beneath a 16th-century Aragonese tower.

name: Mondello
sand: Soft white sand
swimming: Warm, shallow and calm in the sheltered bay
setting: Palermo's belle-époque resort bay between Monte Pellegrino and Monte Gallo
description: Palermo's beloved resort bay, a curve of white sand and turquoise water framed by Liberty-style bathhouses and two mountains.

name: Monterosso al Mare
sand: Coarse sand and pebbles
swimming: Good, gently shelving in the Fegina bay
setting: Pastel village and terraced vineyards of the Cinque Terre
description: The only true sandy beach of the Cinque Terre, fronting a pastel village of the Ligurian coast.

name: Polignano a Mare
sand: Pebbles in a cliff-walled cove
swimming: Deep and clear, dramatic between the ravine walls
setting: A pebble cove wedged in a ravine beneath Polignano's clifftop old town
description: A pebble cove dramatically wedged in a ravine below Polignano's whitewashed clifftop town, famous for cliff-diving.

name: Rimini Beach
sand: Fifteen kilometres of fine groomed sand under regimented rows of umbrellas
water: Warm, shallow and slightly milky Adriatic
swimming: Bath-warm and waist-deep a long way out; ideal for children
setting: Italy's beach-holiday capital since 1843 — numbered bagni, promenades and Fellini-era grand hotels
description: The grand dame of Adriatic resorts — 15 km of groomed sand, numbered umbrellas and dolce vita nightlife.

name: Spiaggia dei Conigli
sand: Fine white
swimming: Shallow, warm, calm
setting: Nature reserve, turtle nesting
description: Rabbit Beach — a turtle-nesting bay of turquoise closer to Africa than Italy.

name: Spiaggia del Principe
sand: Fine pale sand with a faint pink tinge
swimming: Calm, shallow entry, excellent
setting: Granite headlands and maquis of the Emerald Coast
description: The jewel of the Costa Smeralda, a horseshoe cove of emerald water rimmed by pink-flecked sand.

name: Spiaggia della Rotonda
sand: Fine white sand
swimming: Shallow, warm, calm
setting: Below Tropea's clifftop old town and the Santa Maria dell'Isola sanctuary
description: White sand and turquoise water spread beneath Tropea's dramatic clifftop old town.

name: Tropea Beach
sand: Pale golden sand beneath a sheer sandstone cliff
swimming: Warm, clear and gently shelving
setting: The clifftop old town and the rock-perched sanctuary of Santa Maria dell'Isola
description: Calabria's postcard — turquoise water and golden sand beneath Tropea's cliff-hung old town and island sanctuary.

name: Baia dei Turchi
sand: Soft golden-white sand
swimming: Clean and gently shelving
setting: Protected pine woodland behind the dunes
description: A pinewood-backed strand where Ottoman raiders once landed, now a protected turquoise bay.

name: Baia del Silenzio
sand: Fine golden sand
swimming: Sheltered, calm and shallow in the near-enclosed bay
setting: A near-circular 'Bay of Silence' ringed by pastel fishermen's houses
description: The 'Bay of Silence' — an almost-enclosed Ligurian cove of calm water rimmed by a curve of pastel fishermen's houses.

name: Baia delle Zagare
sand: White pebbles and sand
swimming: Deep and clear beneath the cliffs
setting: A cliff-backed bay with towering rock arches on the Gargano promontory
description: A dazzling white cove on the Gargano cliffs, guarded by two towering limestone sea-stack arches rising from the water.

name: Baia di Paraggi
sand: A single pocket of coarse golden sand — the only real sand on the Portofino promontory
water: Startling emerald green over seagrass and sand
swimming: Sheltered, clear and calm inside the Portofino marine protected area
setting: Pine-backed cove on the coast path between Santa Margherita and Portofino, carved into chic beach clubs with one small free corner
description: Portofino's only sandy cove — emerald water, umbrella pines and some of Italy's priciest sunbeds.

name: Cala Brandinchi
sand: Fine soft white sand
swimming: Warm, shallow, family-friendly
setting: Pine and juniper backing, views to Tavolara island
description: Nicknamed Little Tahiti for its powder sand and warm turquoise shallows facing Tavolara.

name: Cala Coticcio
sand: Coarse pale sand and smooth granite
swimming: Deep, clear and calm in the sheltered twin coves
setting: Reached on a rough trail or by boat in the Maddalena archipelago park
description: Nicknamed 'Tahiti', a hidden granite-framed double cove in the Maddalena marine park reached only on foot or by boat.

name: Cala Rossa
sand: White tufa rock ledges and pebbles
swimming: Deep, superb clarity, snorkelling
setting: Old flooded tufa quarries and low cliffs
description: Turquoise water fills the flooded tufa quarries of Favignana's most celebrated cove.

name: Cala Violina
sand: Fine white sand that sings underfoot
swimming: Calm, shallow, clean
setting: Reached on foot through a Mediterranean nature reserve
description: Sand that squeaks like a violin underfoot, reached by a walk through unspoilt Maremma woodland.

name: Calamosche
sand: Soft golden sand
swimming: Sheltered, shallow and clean between two rocky arms
setting: A wild cove reached on a footpath through the Vendicari nature reserve
description: A sheltered golden cove hidden at the end of a reserve footpath, its clear water a favourite of Sicilian snorkellers.

name: Cefalù Beach
sand: Golden sand
swimming: Shallow and gentle right below the old town
setting: A sandy crescent beneath a medieval town and its great Norman cathedral rock
description: A golden crescent right beneath Cefalù's medieval lanes, Norman cathedral and the great crag that towers over the town.

name: Costa Rei
sand: Fine pale sand, almost white, running arrow-straight for kilometres
swimming: Gentle sandy gradient the whole way; easy for children
setting: A long ribbon backed by low dunes and holiday villas, with the flat granite Scoglio di Peppino islet at its southern end
description: Southeast Sardinia's endless ribbon — kilometres of pale sand and shallows ending at the Peppino rock.

name: Fetovaia
sand: Fine golden granite sand
swimming: Sheltered, clear and calm behind a granite spur
setting: A sheltered cove behind a pink-granite headland on Elba's south-west
description: Elba's loveliest cove, a sheltered arc of golden granite sand and clear green water tucked behind a pink-rock spur.

name: Fornillo Beach
sand: Dark sand and pebbles
swimming: Deep and clear, calmer and quieter than the main beach
setting: Positano's quieter second beach, reached by a cliff path past medieval towers
description: Positano's quieter beach, reached along a cliff walkway and framed by the tumbling pastel houses of the Amalfi Coast.

name: Is Arutas
sand: Tiny quartz grains resembling rice
swimming: Open water, can be breezy
setting: Wild protected Sinis coast near a marine reserve
description: A shore made not of sand but of tiny white and pink quartz grains, unique in the Mediterranean.

name: Lido di Venezia
sand: Broad fine golden sand lined with rows of vintage capanne
swimming: Very gradual shelf and tame water; lifeguarded in season
setting: The 12 km barrier island that gave the world the word 'lido'; Piazza San Marco is 15 minutes away by vaporetto
description: The original lido — belle-époque beach huts on calm sand, a boat ride from Piazza San Marco.

name: Pescoluse (Maldive del Salento)
sand: Fine white sand
swimming: Warm, shallow, ideal for families
setting: Low dunes on the Ionian tip of Salento
description: Powder sand and warm shallows earned this Salento strand its nickname, the Maldives of Italy.

name: Poetto
sand: Long pale golden sand
swimming: Shallow and calm along its whole eight-kilometre run
setting: Cagliari's lively city beach below the Sella del Diavolo headland, lined with kiosks
description: Cagliari's eight-kilometre city strand, a summer-long parade of kiosks, kitesurfers and flamingo-filled salt pans.

name: Porto Giunco
sand: Fine white sand

setting: A 180-metre wooden pier and the wooded, crumbling Orłowo Cliff at Gdynia's genteel southern edge, one train stop from Sopot
description: Tricity's most painterly shore — a wooden pier and fishing boats beneath the wooded Orłowo cliff.

name: Międzyzdroje Beach
sand: Wide golden sand beneath green cliffs
swimming: Gentle and lifeguarded along the pier
setting: A belle-époque resort with a long pier, between the sea and Wolin National Park's cliffs
description: A belle-époque Baltic resort where golden sand runs beneath the green moraine cliffs of Wolin National Park.

name: Sopot Beach
sand: Broad white sand along the Gulf of Gdansk
swimming: Shallow and gentle, popular family swimming
setting: A lively spa resort with Europe's longest wooden pier, the molo
description: Poland's chicest resort, where a grand wooden pier reaches over the Baltic beside broad white sand.

name: Świnoujście Beach
sand: Poland's widest beach — a deep apron of fine pale sand raked flat each morning
water: Clear grey-green Baltic over a pale sandy bottom
swimming: Very gradual shelf and gentle sandbars; guarded bathing sections in season
setting: Fronts the spa quarter by the Świna mouth and its windmill-shaped Stawa Młyny beacon; the promenade runs west into German Ahlbeck
description: Poland's widest beach, where a deep apron of fine sand and its seafront promenade run unbroken into Germany.

name: Dębki Beach
sand: Wide soft white-grey sand under low grassy dunes
swimming: Sandbars and light open-sea chop; lifeguarded stretch in high summer
setting: Where the little Piaśnica river crosses the sand into the sea — the stream once marked Poland's interwar border with Germany; pine woods and a low-key holiday hamlet behind
description: A Kashubian pine-and-dune hamlet where the Piaśnica slides across white sand into a bracing Baltic.

name: Hel Peninsula Beach
sand: Fine white sand
water: Shallow, clear Baltic blue on two sides
swimming: Shallow, calm and clean; open sea on one side, calm bay on the other
surf: The bay side is a leading windsurf and kite spot
setting: A slender 35-kilometre sand spit with sea on one side and calm bay on the other
description: A slender 35-kilometre sand spit with open Baltic surf on one side and a flat windsurfer's bay on the other.

name: Kołobrzeg Beach
sand: Wide, well-kept pale sand
swimming: Gently shelving and lifeguarded by the pier
setting: Poland's salt-spa city with a lighthouse, port and grand promenade
description: Poland's classic spa-town beach — wide pale sand between a working lighthouse and a century of health resorts.

name: Łeba Beach
sand: Fine white sand and giant dunes
swimming: Shallow and gently warming in summer
setting: A white-sand resort beside the shifting dunes of Słowiński National Park
description: A fine white-sand Baltic beach beside the vast wandering dunes of Słowiński National Park — Poland's 'moving desert'.

name: Ustka Beach
sand: Fine pale sand below low dunes
swimming: Gently shelving, bracing outside high summer
surf: Small Baltic beach-break on windy days
setting: A fishing port turned resort near the shifting dunes of Słowiński park
description: A Pomeranian fishing port's pale strand, a quieter cousin to the great shifting dunes along the coast.

name: Benagil Beach & Cave
sand: Golden
swimming: Calm cove
setting: Domed sea cave with skylight
description: A cathedral-like sea cave with a sandy floor and a natural skylight.

name: Ilhéu de Vila Franca do Campo
locality: Vila Franca do Campo, São Miguel
sand: No sand — you swim in a flooded volcanic crater ringed by black rock
water: Clear blue-green, sheltered from the Atlantic swell inside the crater walls
swimming: A calm, near-circular natural pool reached by ferry, with daily visitor limits in summer
setting: A tiny sunken volcano off São Miguel's south coast — a protected nature reserve forming an almost perfect round lagoon
description: A drowned volcano off São Miguel makes an almost perfect circular pool — the Azores' most photographed swim, reached only by ferry.

name: Praia da Comporta
sand: Miles of fine white sand and dunes
swimming: Open Atlantic with some waves and currents
setting: Rice paddies and umbrella-pine dunes
description: An endless, dune-backed white strand fringing the rice fields of chic, laid-back Comporta.

name: Praia da Marinha
sand: Golden
swimming: Clear, some swell
setting: Ochre limestone cliffs
description: The Algarve’s emblem — golden cliffs and sea stacks over clear water.

name: Praia de Porto Santo
sand: 9km of fine golden mineral-rich sand
swimming: Calm and shallow, excellent swimming
setting: A low golden island off the coast of Madeira
description: Nine kilometres of fine golden sand famed for its therapeutic minerals on Madeira's sunny sister island.

name: Praia do Guincho
sand: Wide pale sand backed by dunes
swimming: Cold, powerful surf and strong wind
surf: Consistent Atlantic beach break and world windsurf venue
setting: Dunes below the Sintra hills near Cabo da Roca
description: A wild, wind-raked Atlantic beach below the Sintra mountains and a mecca for surfers and windsurfers.

name: Praia do Norte
sand: Golden
swimming: Dangerous when big
setting: Canyon-fed swell
description: Where an undersea canyon builds the largest surfed waves ever ridden.

name: Praia do Supertubos
sand: Dark-blond sand packed hard by a heaving shore-dump
water: Cold, glassy green with spray hanging over the lineup on offshore days
swimming: Not for swimming — hollow shorebreak and strong currents
surf: 'Europe's Pipeline': thick sand-bottom barrels and a fixture of the World Surf League championship tour
setting: An open strand just south of Peniche's harbour jetty, low dunes and canning-town outskirts behind
description: Europe's Pipeline — a thick, freight-train sandbar barrel that decides world-tour heats.

name: Ilha de Tavira
sand: Eleven kilometres of fine white-gold sand and low dunes
water: Calm, clear and reliably among the warmest on Portugal's coast
swimming: Gentle shelf and small waves, lifeguards near the landing; crowds thin ten minutes' walk out
setting: A car-free Ria Formosa barrier island reached by ferry or water-taxi from Tavira's quay
description: A car-free barrier island of warm, calm sea ten minutes by ferry from Tavira.

name: Machico Beach
sand: Golden sand brought from Morocco, filling a calm town bay
water: Sheltered, clear and calm within the horseshoe bay
swimming: Warm and calm, gently shelving and lifeguarded — good for families
setting: The golden town beach of Machico, Madeira's first capital, in a sheltered valley bay on the east coast
description: Madeira's first capital gave itself a beach — golden sand shipped from Morocco to fill a calm, sheltered town bay.

name: Porto Pim Beach
sand: Dark volcanic sand and fine shingle in a sheltered horseshoe bay
water: Calm, clear and cool, protected inside a volcanic amphitheatre
swimming: Sheltered and calm — the safest, warmest swimming on Faial
setting: A curved dark-sand bay beside the historic whaling town of Horta, framed by the green Monte da Guia headland
description: A sheltered dark-sand bay hugging the old whaling town of Horta, cupped by a green volcanic headland on Faial.

name: Praia da Calheta
sand: Golden sand shipped in from the Sahara, held between two breakwaters
water: Calm, clear and turquoise inside the sheltered twin lagoons
swimming: Warm, calm and shallow — Madeira's easiest and safest swimming
setting: Two man-made golden-sand lagoons on Madeira's sunny south-west coast, beside a marina below banana terraces

name: Praia da Falesia

swimming: Generally calm and gently shelving
setting: Towering ochre-and-red striped cliffs
description: A six-kilometre ribbon of sand beneath spectacular ochre and rust-red cliffs on the central Algarve.

name: Praia da Luz
sand: Pale golden sand shelving gently below a palm-lined promenade
water: Clear teal, usually flat by south-coast standards
swimming: Sheltered, lifeguarded and easy — the western Algarve's classic family swim
setting: A whitewashed ex-fishing village walled in at its east end by the dark volcanic slab of Rocha Negra
description: A sheltered golden bay beneath the dark volcanic headland of Rocha Negra.

name: Praia da Rocha
sand: Wide golden sand
swimming: Broad, gently shelving and lifeguarded
setting: The Algarve's grand resort beach beneath a promenade and ochre rock formations
description: The Algarve's original resort beach — a vast golden strand of ochre stacks below the bars and promenade of Portimão.

name: Praia de Miramar
sand: Pale gold sand broken by smooth granite outcrops
water: Chilly, clear north Atlantic with steady small breakers
swimming: Bracing; lifeguarded in season, with calmer pools among the rocks at low tide
surf: Small, consistent beach-break peaks; a local longboard favourite
setting: The seventeenth-century Senhor da Pedra chapel stands on a wave-washed granite boss mid-strand, twenty minutes from Porto by local train
description: The beach where waves wash a baroque chapel — Senhor da Pedra on its granite rock.

name: Praia de Santa Barbara
sand: Dark volcanic sand
swimming: Lively surf and currents, a surfers' beach
setting: Green volcanic hills on Sao Miguel's north coast
description: A long black-sand beach on Sao Miguel's north coast and the Azores' leading surf destination.

name: Praia do Amado
sand: Broad golden sand
swimming: Cool with waves; watch for currents
surf: Reliable beach break and surf-school favourite
setting: The rugged cliffs of the Costa Vicentina
description: A golden, cliff-backed surf beach on the wild Costa Vicentina and one of the Algarve's top wave spots.

name: Praia do Baleal
sand: A soft cream arc of sand joining Baleal islet to the mainland
swimming: Pick your side — the south bay is sheltered and swimmable, the north side takes the swell
surf: Mellow sandbar rollers make it Peniche's surf-school nursery, with punchier reef peaks off the islet
setting: A causeway tombolo below the old whalers' hamlet on Baleal islet, surf camps stacked behind the dunes
description: A two-faced tombolo — sheltered swimming on one side, learner waves on the other, Baleal islet at the tip.

name: Praia do Beliche
sand: Soft pale sand pocketed beneath fifty-metre ochre cliffs
swimming: Sheltered from the summer northerlies and swimmable on calm days; respect any swell
surf: Sagres' quality wave — hollow peaks when northwest swell wraps between the capes
setting: A long stair-climb down between Sagres point and Cabo de São Vicente, under the tiny Beliche fort
description: A cliff-hidden wedge of sand on Europe's south-west corner, glowing gold at sunset.

name: Praia do Camilo
sand: Golden
swimming: Sheltered, clear
setting: Twin coves, wooden stairway
description: Twin golden coves reached by a photogenic wooden stairway.

name: Praia Dona Ana
sand: Golden sand between ochre cliffs
swimming: Sheltered, calm and clear among the rock stacks
setting: A sheltered cove of golden cliffs and sea stacks near Lagos
description: The Algarve's postcard cove — golden ochre cliffs and honey-coloured sea stacks framing calm, clear water near Lagos.

name: Praia dos Galapinhos
sand: Fine pale sand below green Arrábida slopes
water: Astonishingly clear green-turquoise for the Atlantic
swimming: Sheltered, calm and clear under the ridge
setting: The Arrábida natural park's forest tumbling straight into the sea
description: Portugal's clearest water — a turquoise Atlantic anomaly beneath the green ridge of Arrábida.

name: Costa Nova
sand: Wide pale sand and dunes
swimming: Bracing Atlantic swell; lifeguarded in summer
setting: A dune beach behind a row of candy-striped fishermen's houses near Aveiro
description: A breezy dune beach fronted by Portugal's famous row of candy-striped wooden fishermen's houses near Aveiro.

name: Praia da Adraga
sand: Golden sand below dark cliffs
swimming: Powerful Atlantic swell and currents; a dramatic swim
setting: A wild cliff-walled beach with a great offshore rock arch near Sintra
description: A wild beach walled by dark cliffs and crowned by a great offshore rock arch on the Atlantic edge of Sintra.

name: Praia da Arrifana
sand: Golden sand below a cliff
swimming: Lively swell; a renowned surf bay
surf: A sheltered, consistent point and beach-break on the west coast
setting: A cliff-cradled bay below a clifftop village and old fort
description: A cliff-cradled surf bay below a whitewashed village and ruined fort — the heart of the west-coast surf scene.

name: Praia da Bordeira
sand: Vast pale sand and dunes
swimming: Strong currents — a surf and dune-walking beach
surf: Long, consistent beach-break on the wild south-west
setting: A huge dune-and-river beach in the Costa Vicentina wild park
description: A vast, wild sweep of sand and dunes on the untamed Costa Vicentina, one of the Algarve's great surf and walking beaches.

name: Praia da Foz do Arelho
sand: A huge sand tongue between lagoon and ocean
water: Warm, calm lagoon on one side; cold Atlantic surf on the other
swimming: Children in the lagoon, swimmers in the sea — pick your side
setting: Where the Óbidos lagoon opens to the Atlantic
description: Two beaches in one — a warm shallow lagoon lapping one side of the sand, the cold Atlantic pounding the other.

name: Praia da Ursa
sand: Golden
swimming: Dangerous currents
setting: Sea stacks near Europe’s westmost point
description: A wild, hard-won cove of sea stacks beside continental Europe’s edge.

name: Praia da Zambujeira do Mar
sand: Pale sand walled by dark schist cliffs
swimming: Swim close in on calm days; respect the shorebreak
setting: A whitewashed clifftop village on the wild Alentejo coast
description: The Alentejo's cliff-walled village beach — whitewashed houses above, cold wild Atlantic below.

name: Praia de Cacela Velha
sand: Bare white-gold barrier-island sand with nothing built on it
water: Gin-clear jade — lagoon-warmed, the mildest sea in the Algarve
swimming: Bath-warm shallows at low tide; mind the pull in the lagoon inlet channel

description: The eastern Algarve's quiet miracle — an empty barrier strand below a tiny walled hamlet.

name: Praia de Odeceixe
sand: Golden sand where river meets sea
water: Calm river water beside cool surf sea
swimming: Warm calm river or bracing Atlantic surf
surf: Good beach break on the sea side
setting: A horseshoe river mouth framed by green cliffs
description: A striking horseshoe beach at a river mouth offering calm river swimming beside the wild Atlantic surf.

name: Praia de São Rafael
sand: Soft golden sand
swimming: Sheltered, shallow and calm among the rocks
setting: A small cove of sculpted golden rock stacks west of Albufeira
description: A small, sheltered cove where sculpted golden rock arches and stacks rise from calm, clear water near Albufeira.

name: Praia do Martinhal
sand: Fine golden sand
water: Clear, sheltered blue with offshore islets
swimming: Calm and clear, family-friendly at Europe's south-west corner
surf: Gentle waves and a windsurf spot
setting: A sheltered family bay with rocky islets near windswept Sagres
description: A sheltered, islet-dotted family bay near the wild headlands of Sagres at the very south-west corner of Europe.

name: Praia do Seixal

setting: A black-sand beach beneath green cliffs and waterfalls on Madeira's north
description: A jet-black volcanic beach beneath the green cliffs and tumbling waterfalls of Madeira's dramatic north coast.

description: Romania's summer capital — a sandbar of pale sand and beach clubs squeezed between a lagoon and the Black Sea.

description: Where the Danube finally meets the Black Sea — a boat-only delta village with an immense, empty beach.

sand: Coarse pale sand
swimming: Open-sea swimming straight off the village
setting: A legendary bohemian village on the Bulgarian border, famous for all-night beach parties
description: Romania's counter-culture beach — a fishing village turned all-night bohemian institution on the last sand before Bulgaria.

sand: A vast, empty strand of soft grey-gold sand

setting: The last wild beach before the Danube Delta biosphere, no buildings in sight
description: Romania's emptiest beach — kilometres of bare sand running north toward the Danube Delta, with nothing built on it at all.

name: Eforie Nord
sand: Fine pale sand below low bluffs
water: Clean, gently shelving Black Sea water
swimming: Gentle and family-friendly, with mud-spa lakes behind
setting: An old-school spa resort between the sea and the therapeutic Techirghiol mud lake
description: A century-old Romanian spa resort where a pale-sand beach meets the famous black mud of Lake Techirghiol.

description: Vama Veche's mellower sibling — an old fishing village where Bucharest's bohemia summers on shell-flecked sand.

name: Falsterbo Beach

swimming: Family-calm — you can wade a long way out

description: White bathing huts and long warm shallows on the Swedish Riviera at the country's south-west corner.

description: Malmö's mile-long city beach — shallow Öresund water, grassy lawns and a row of wooden bathing jetties, with Copenhagen on the horizon.

name: Sandhammaren

swimming: Gentle Baltic swimming; watch for offshore winds
setting: Wild dunes and pine forest on Sweden's southeastern corner
description: Sweden's celebrated white-sand shore, a wild ribbon of dunes on the country's sunny southeast corner.

name: Tylösand
sand: Wide pale sand
swimming: Shallow, calm and comparatively warm for Sweden
setting: Sweden's best-known resort beach on the Halland coast
description: Sweden's favourite summer resort beach — seven kilometres of pale sand and shallow water on the sunny Halland coast.

name: Åhus Täppet
sand: A wide belt of fine pale sand backed by pine forest
water: Shallow, relatively warm Hanö Bay Baltic
swimming: Shallow, gentle and family-friendly, among the Baltic's warmest water
setting: The popular Täppet beach south of Åhus, backed by dunes and pine forest on Sweden's south-east coast
description: A wide, pine-backed family beach on Sweden's south-east coast, with some of the warmest, shallowest water in the Baltic.

sand: A wide, flat sand-and-shingle beach that empties at low water

surf: Sweden's premier windsurfing and kitesurfing bay, with steady Kattegat wind
