description: Sweden's windsurfing capital — a wide, wind-swept Kattegat bay south of Varberg where boards outnumber bathers.

name: Böda Sand
sand: Fine pale sand
swimming: Shallow, calm and gently warming in high summer
setting: The longest sandy beach on the sunny island of Öland, backed by pine forest
description: The longest sandy beach on sun-soaked Öland — kilometres of pale sand and shallow Baltic water backed by pines.

name: Skanör Beach
sand: Pale sand and dunes
swimming: Shallow, calm and warm for the region
setting: Wide dune-backed sands at Sweden's south-western tip, a birdwatching haven
description: Wide dune-backed sands at Sweden's south-west corner, where shallow warm water meets one of Europe's great birding capes.

name: Sudersand Beach
sand: A three-kilometre crescent of fine pale sand
swimming: Shallow and gentle on a gently curving bay
setting: Bergman's stark island of Fårö, its gentlest side
description: The soft side of Ingmar Bergman's stark Fårö — a three-kilometre crescent of pale Baltic sand.

name: Tofta Beach
sand: Long, soft pale sand — rare for rocky Gotland
water: Shallow, clear and quick to warm
swimming: Shallow and family-friendly, Gotland's warmest water
setting: Gotland's summer-party sand, twenty minutes from medieval Visby
description: Gotland's summer institution — a long shallow strand where Stockholm decamps every July, near medieval Visby.

name: Moon Bay (Mesecev Zaliv)
sand: Shingle and mud-sand flats
swimming: Shallow and calm over tidal flats
setting: Beneath the highest flysch cliffs on the Adriatic, in a nature reserve
description: Slovenia's wildest beach curls beneath the Adriatic's tallest cliffs in the Strunjan reserve.

name: Fiesa Beach
sand: Pebbles
swimming: Calm and clear in the sheltered bay beside a small lagoon
setting: A quiet pebble cove a short cliff walk from the Venetian town of Piran
description: A quiet pebble cove just around the cliff from Venetian Piran, Slovenia's prettiest and least-crowded swim spot.

name: Portorož Beach
sand: Groomed imported sand and lawns
swimming: Warm and family-calm in the protected bay
setting: Slovenia's belle-époque 'Port of Roses' spa resort
description: Slovenia's Port of Roses — a groomed belle-époque resort beach on the warm, calm top of the Adriatic.

name: Strunjan Beach

water: Calm, warm and shallow inside the sheltered bay

description: Slovenia's calmest swim — a nature-park shore between working salt pans and the Adriatic's highest cliffs.

description: A wild sandbar between sea and river delta, one of the Mediterranean's key loggerhead turtle nurseries.

name: Ölüdeniz (Blue Lagoon)
sand: White pebble-sand

setting: Nature-park lagoon
description: A sheltered turquoise lagoon with paragliders drifting down from above.

sand: Eighteen kilometres of golden sand backed by dunes
swimming: Warm and open, some surf and current on windy days
setting: Protected national park with ancient Lycian ruins and loggerhead turtle nesting
description: Turkey's longest beach, eighteen wild kilometres of dune-backed sand guarded by Lycian ruins and nesting sea turtles.

name: Akyaka Beach
sand: A short strand of pale sand and river greenery

swimming: Gentle by the river mouth; the gulf is kite country
surf: One of Europe's great kitesurf flats at nearby Gökova
setting: A slow-town of Ottoman-style timber houses where a cold river meets the gulf
description: A slow-living timber-house town where an ice-cold river meets the Gökova kite flats.

name: Ayazma Beach

water: Brisk, crystal-clear turquoise below pale bluffs
swimming: Refreshingly cool and clean; the meltemi can chop up afternoons
setting: Bozcaada's flagship south-coast beach, cafés on the sand and the 1734 Aya Paraskevi monastery with its holy spring on the slope above

description: A hidden turquoise cove at the mouth of a sheer canyon reached by boat, home to clouds of rare butterflies.

name: Cirali Beach
sand: Long pebble-and-sand beach

swimming: Warm and clear, deepens fairly quickly
setting: Backed by mountains, the ancient ruins of Olympos and the eternal Chimaera flames, a turtle nesting shore
description: A tranquil pebble shore backed by pine mountains, ancient Olympos and the eternal flames of the Chimaera.

name: Cleopatra Beach
sand: Fine soft golden sand
swimming: Warm, gentle and gently shelving, excellent
setting: Beneath Alanya's Seljuk castle headland, lined with a lively resort promenade
description: A soft golden crescent below Alanya's medieval castle, a lively Blue Flag beach lapped by warm turquoise water.

name: Cleopatra Beach (Sedir Island)

description: An island beach of rare spherical oolitic sand so protected that shoes and towels are forbidden on the grains themselves.

name: Ilica Beach
sand: Wide fine white-gold sand

swimming: Very shallow, warm and calm, a family favourite
setting: A lively Aegean resort strip with thermal spa hotels near Cesme
description: A broad Aegean resort beach where warm thermal springs bubble up into shallow turquoise shallows near Cesme.

name: Kaputaş Beach
sand: Pebble-sand
swimming: Warm, deep-ish
setting: Foot of a gorge, cliff steps
description: A jewel of turquoise at the mouth of a gorge, reached down 190 steps.

name: Konyaaltı Beach
sand: Grey pebbles
swimming: Clear and quickly deepening, cooled by mountain streams
setting: Antalya's long city beach beneath the snow-topped Taurus Mountains
description: Antalya's long pebble city beach, backed by a green promenade and the snow-capped wall of the Taurus Mountains.

name: Phaselis Beach
sand: Pine-shaded pebble-sand coves
swimming: Swim off a 2,300-year-old harbour mole
setting: Three bays of a ruined Lycian port city beneath Mount Tahtalı
description: Swim in the harbours of a ruined Lycian city — pines, marble streets and turquoise water under Mount Tahtalı.

name: Sarımsaklı Beach

setting: Ayvalık's resort quarter at Küçükköy — 7 km of shore with Greek Lesbos on the horizon and olive country behind
description: Seven kilometres of fine yellow sand below Ayvalık's olive hills, with Lesbos floating offshore.

name: Side Kumköy Beach

swimming: Sandy-bottomed and gently shelving; tame, family-friendly water
setting: The resort strip 4 km west of Side's peninsula, joined to the Roman theatre and Temple of Apollo by a seafront promenade
description: Side's sandy resort mile — all-inclusive ease with a Roman theatre and the Temple of Apollo a promenade walk away.

name: Adrasan Beach
sand: Golden sand grading to small pebbles along a 2 km bay
water: Pool-calm, transparent blue-green behind sheltering headlands
swimming: Mountain-sheltered and often waveless — some of the calmest swimming on the Lycian coast
setting: A sleepy garden-village bay on the Lycian Way between Olympos and the Gelidonya lighthouse, pine mountains on three sides
description: The Lycian coast at half speed — a pool-calm, pine-ringed bay between Olympos and the Gelidonya lighthouse.

name: Altınkum Beach
sand: Fine 'golden sand'
swimming: Warm, shallow and calm — great for families
setting: A string of shallow golden coves on the breezy Çeşme peninsula
description: The 'golden sands' of Çeşme — a string of shallow turquoise coves whose afternoon breeze also draws windsurfers.

name: Bitez Beach
sand: Coarse golden sand and jetty platforms
water: Calm, clear and warm in a sheltered bay
swimming: Warm and windless — Bodrum's gentlest bay
surf: None; a windsurf-school bay in the afternoons
setting: Tangerine groves running almost to the water on the Bodrum peninsula
description: Bodrum's mellowest bay — warm sheltered water fringed by tangerine groves and low-key beach clubs.

name: Gümüşlük Beach
sand: Pebbles and sand
water: Clear, calm blue over sunken ruins
swimming: Calm and clear; wade toward the sunken city of ancient Myndos
setting: A bohemian fishing village of seafood tables over the sunken ruins of Myndos
description: A bohemian Bodrum village where seafood tables line a calm bay above the sunken ruins of ancient Myndos.

name: Kabak Beach
sand: Sand-and-shingle cove
swimming: Warm, clear and calm in the bay
setting: A remote forested valley below Faralya, backed by pine cliffs and eco-camps
description: A brilliant turquoise cove beneath forested cliffs, a bohemian off-grid escape at the end of the Lycian trail.

name: Lara Beach
sand: Fine golden sand
swimming: Warm and gently shelving
setting: A long sandy resort strip of grand themed hotels east of Antalya
description: Antalya's golden-sand resort strip, a long warm-water beach lined with the city's grandest all-inclusive hotels.

name: Ovabükü
sand: Fine sand and pebbles
swimming: Clear, calm and deep-shelving
setting: A laid-back cove of small pensions on the remote Datça peninsula
description: A laid-back turquoise cove of family pensions and almond groves on the long, remote Datça peninsula.

name: Palamutbükü
sand: Small white pebbles fringed by tamarisk and almond trees
water: Gin-clear and noticeably fresh — one stretch is nicknamed 'the aquarium'
swimming: Clean pebble entry, superb visibility, famously calm
setting: A low-key strip of fish restaurants and pansiyons on the Datça peninsula's south shore, olive groves behind
description: Datça's slow-life shore — white pebbles, aquarium-clear water and unhurried waterfront fish tavernas.

name: Suluada
sand: White sand on an uninhabited islet
swimming: Warm, shallow and calm off the boat-only islet
setting: An uninhabited island reached by boat from Adrasan
description: The 'Turkish Maldives' — an uninhabited islet of white sand and luminous turquoise reached by boat from Adrasan.
